summary: Azure Monitor is Microsoft's unified observability service that collects, analyzes, and acts on telemetry (metrics, logs, traces, events) from Azure and hybrid environments. It brings together data from all Azure resources into a centralized platform supporting tools like Log Analytics, Application Insights, and alerting. It enables monitoring of applications, infrastructure, networks, and AI agents from a single service.

key_concepts.explanation: A centralized repository that stores log and trace data from Azure resources, queried using Kusto Query Language (KQL)
An Azure Monitor feature providing application performance monitoring (APM) for live web applications using OpenTelemetry
Metrics are numerical time-series data stored in Azure Monitor workspaces (queried with PromQL); logs are structured records stored in Log Analytics workspaces (queried with KQL)
Proactive notifications triggered when collected data meets defined conditions, with AIOps features like dynamic thresholds and smart alerts
An Azure Monitor feature that automatically adds or removes resources based on application load
Configuration objects that define what telemetry to collect from which sources and where to send it
Interactive reports in the Azure portal that visualize monitoring data with prebuilt or custom layouts
A suite of tools built on the Azure Monitor data platform for monitoring network health
Azure Monitor can monitor non-Azure resources via Azure Arc, extending to other clouds and on-premises environments

exam_focus_points: Know that Log Analytics uses KQL (Kusto Query Language) and Azure Monitor workspaces use PromQL for Prometheus metrics
Understand that Application Insights is a feature of Azure Monitor, not a separate service
Know when to use Alerts vs Autoscale: Alerts notify humans, Autoscale automatically adjusts resources
Understand Data Collection Rules (DCRs) as the mechanism to control what data is collected and where it goes
Know that Log Analytics workspaces store logs/traces, while Azure Monitor workspaces store Prometheus/OpenTelemetry metrics
Azure Monitor supports hybrid environments via Azure Arc integration
Diagnostic settings must be configured on individual resources to send logs/metrics to Log Analytics
Know the difference between metric alerts (near real-time) and log alerts (query-based, slight delay)
similar_concepts: Log Analytics Workspace vs Azure Monitor Workspace
Azure Monitor Alerts vs Azure Service Health Alerts
Diagnostic Settings vs Data Collection Rules
Azure Monitor vs Microsoft Defender for Cloud
common_mistakes: Confusing Log Analytics Workspace (for logs, uses KQL) with Azure Monitor Workspace (for Prometheus metrics, uses PromQL)
Assuming Azure Monitor only works with Azure resources — it also supports hybrid and multi-cloud via Azure Arc
Thinking Application Insights is separate from Azure Monitor — it is a feature within Azure Monitor
Forgetting that diagnostic settings must be explicitly enabled on each resource; monitoring is not automatic
Confusing Autoscale (adjusts resource count automatically) with Alerts (sends notifications to humans)
Assuming all Azure Monitor data is queryable with KQL — Prometheus metrics require PromQL
